name: AtomS3 character-LCD status panel
description: M5Stack AtomS3 driving a 20x4 HD44780 character LCD over its Grove I2C port via a PCF8574 backpack. The tiny S3 cube sits behind the panel and renders status text on the big, readable character display -- a cheap always-on status board.

requirements.text: render status text on a 20x4 character LCD
LCD is 5V HD44780 on a PCF8574 backpack; drive it from the AtomS3 Grove I2C port (GPIO1/GPIO2)

warnings.text: The PCF8574 backpack pulls SDA/SCL to its 5V VCC, but the ESP32-S3 GPIOs are not 5V-tolerant. Most Grove LCD units work in practice because I2C is open-drain and the bus rarely swings to a hard 5V, but for a clean design add a level shifter or pull the bus to 3V3.
